message:
feat(vdbe): reject unrewritten IN subqueries at codegen boundary

Shift the IN (SELECT ...) / IN table evaluation strategy: instead of
attempting inline scan-probe codegen for subquery IN sets, the VDBE
codegen now returns an Unsupported error if subqueries reach it without
prior rewrite. This enforces a clean separation where the connection-layer
rewriter handles subquery evaluation and codegen only sees literal IN lists.

Key changes:
- Add contains_unsupported_in_expr/contains_unsupported_in_result_column
  recursive walkers that detect unrewritten IN subquery/table references
  across all expression contexts (SELECT columns, WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING,
  ORDER BY, UPDATE SET, DELETE WHERE)
- Guard codegen_select, codegen_insert_select, codegen_update, and
  codegen_delete with upfront unsupported-IN checks
- Add schema field to ScanContext for future typed codegen use
- Suppress clippy::too_many_lines on connection.rs rewrite_in_expr and
  codegen_insert_select
- Update tests from asserting scan-probe opcodes to asserting Unsupported
  errors, remove resolve_in_probe_source test (no longer needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
